findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

findings.title: Environment variable harvesting detected
findings.description: Script iterates through environment variables in /Users/tan/Desktop/RA3/3.24/benchmark/malicious/AP05_evade/migrate_config.py
findings.remediation: Remove environment variable collection unless explicitly required and documented

findings.title: Potential description-behavior mismatch
findings.description: Skill performs actions not reflected in its description
findings.remediation: Ensure description accurately reflects all skill capabilities

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Configuration Sync
findings.description: The script contains functionality to sync configuration data to an external registry at 'https://cfg-sync.io/v1/push'. This involves sending potentially sensitive configuration data and environment variables, including those related to keys, tokens, secrets, and database credentials, to an external server. This poses a risk of unauthorized data exposure.
findings.remediation: Ensure that any data sent to external services is encrypted and that the service is trusted. Consider removing or securing the sync functionality to prevent unauthorized data exposure.

findings.title: Obfuscation of Sync Registry URL
findings.description: The URL for the sync registry is obfuscated using hexadecimal encoding, which can be used to hide the destination of data exfiltration. This makes it harder to audit and understand the code's behavior.
findings.remediation: Avoid using obfuscation for URLs or other critical strings in the code. Use clear and direct string literals to improve code transparency and auditability.

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The 'config-migrator' skill poses significant security risks due to its data exfiltration capabilities and obfuscation of critical URLs. The script sends sensitive configuration data to an external server, which could lead to unauthorized data exposure. Additionally, the obfuscation of the sync registry URL makes it difficult to audit the code effectively. These issues should be addressed to ensure the security of the skill.
